results.title: How Reality Fracture Will Push The Boundaries Of Magic The Gathering (And Its Printers)
results.description: One of Magic the Gathering's biggest releases of 2026, Reality Fracture, will see the franchise's iconic Planeswalker Jace create the Echoverse , a mirrored version of the current MTG universe in which world-defining events have been rewritten. In a press Q&A at this year's MagicCon Vegas, Magic head designer Mark Rosewater said that a "What If" set has been one of the most requested by fans in the franchise's history, and Reality Fracture satisfies that need. However, while the Echoverse creates a ton of possibilities, this release will intentionally be very focused. "Very early on, we were talking about maybe going broader," Rosewater said. "But we decided that one of the lessons from March of the Machines was we went a little bit too broad, and we decided we wanted to focus a little bit more. So, we'll see nods about [other] places, but really, the story is on Hexhaven." Hexhaven is Reality Fracture's version of Strixhaven, which was the focus of the latest Magic release Secrets of Strixhaven . Typically, when one corner of the MTG multiverse is explored in a set, the subsequent releases will revolve around a completely different part of the lore, making Wizard of the Coast's choice to return to the college an unusual one for the game; this decision could easily risk inundating players with too much scholarly content. However, Rosewater reassured me that while Hexhaven is central to Reality Fracture, this set will be different from them doing a Hexhaven-focused set. Continue Reading at GameSpot
creator: tom caswell
